summary: Verified US-844 score/results accessibility for the current score panel, deal-result panel, and final-result panel. Spexus current_context was read before task and epic hierarchy access. Dependency reports PERSONAL-TASK-1153 through PERSONAL-TASK-1156 were present and successful. Source and DOM tests show the score display uses labeled score sections with explicit bullet, mountain, and mutual-whist text; deal and final result panels use labeled regions, headings, structured tables/lists, and understandable result copy; native button controls remain keyboard reachable in active states; and finished bullets expose no settle/next-deal continuation controls. Focused verification and the release gate passed.

tests_run.command: npm run test -- src/ui/render.test.ts src/ui/controller.test.ts src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts
tests_run.evidence: 3 test files passed, 28 tests passed. Covered score panel labels/content, result panel structure/content, legal action controls, discard button/toggle labels, keyboard-order-sensitive resume flow, and finished-state absence of continuation controls.
Vitest: 13 files and 125 tests passed. Build: tsc and vite build completed successfully.

verification_environment.verification_scope: US-844 accessibility review for score table, deal result, final result, and interactive control presence/focus order using existing project verification entrypoints

node_package_scripts.verify: npm run test && npm run build

verification_environment.notes: No production code was changed. Verification used source inspection, existing DOM/component/integration tests, and the repo release gate. No external accessibility scanner exists in the project entrypoints, so the review stayed within existing tests/source and listed visual artifacts.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: src/ui/render.ts scoreboardMarkup; src/styles.css score-panel/score-list rules; src/ui/render.test.ts compact score panel test; npm run test -- src/ui/render.test.ts src/ui/controller.test.ts src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: The compact score display is built from labeled sections and definition lists rather than one native table; this is acceptable for readability and accessible names but is not literal table semantics.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: src/ui/render.ts renderDealResultPanel; src/ui/render.test.ts structured deal result details test; src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts settlement scenario; output/playwright/mz403-settlement.js; output/playwright/mz-403-settlement.png
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: The panel is exposed as a labeled section with h2/h3 structure and native score-change table, but there is no live-region announcement for dynamic updates.
quality_gate_matrix.accepted_risk: No live-region risk accepted for this static render flow because the task requirement is understandable result structure and existing tests verify the rendered state after transition.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: src/ui/render.ts renderFinalResultPanel; src/ui/render.test.ts finished-state final settlement/ranking test; src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts completed bullet scenario; output/playwright/mz403-final.js; output/playwright/mz-403-final.png
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: The final result is a labeled section with h2/h3 structure and an ordered ranking list, but not an aria-live announcement.
quality_gate_matrix.accepted_risk: No blocking risk; finished state is rendered as the primary current state and contains understandable text plus no continuation controls.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: src/ui/render.ts renderActions/actionButton/humanCards/setupDiscardSelection; src/styles.css focus-visible rules; src/ui/render.test.ts legal controls, labels, card enablement, finished-state tests; src/ui/controller.test.ts resume keyboard order test
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Focus order is verified by DOM order and native button behavior; no browser tab-walk automation was added because the task required existing project entrypoints only.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: npm run test -- src/ui/render.test.ts src/ui/controller.test.ts src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts; npm run verify
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Local deterministic test/build evidence for a static browser app; no backend/live API applies.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: PERSONAL-TASK-1157 scope; PERSONAL-EP-180 static app scope; src/ui/render.ts; src/styles.css
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: This verification covers local UI rendering and tests only; no auth, backend, network, or sensitive data path is involved.

evidence_matrix.scenario: The current score display exposes bullet, mountain, and mutual whist values for all three players with readable labels.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/ui/render.ts scoreboardMarkup; src/ui/render.test.ts renders a compact visible score panel with bullet, mountain, and mutual whists for every player; npm run test -- src/ui/render.test.ts src/ui/controller.test.ts src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts
evidence_matrix.limitations: DOM/source review and jsdom assertions; no external screen reader transcript.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Deal and bullet completion results are exposed to the UI as structured result panels with summaries, score changes, ranking, and final settlement text.
evidence_matrix.limitations: In-process renderer/integration coverage, not a full browser assistive-technology audit.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Given a deal completes, when the result is settled, the score table shows updated bullet, mountain, and mutual whist values for all players and the deal result has understandable score-change details.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts settles a deal into next-deal score totals and keeps the updated score/result UI in sync; src/ui/render.test.ts structured deal result details test
evidence_matrix.limitations: Assertions validate rendered DOM text and control presence in jsdom; visual artifacts were reviewed as supporting evidence only.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Given the bullet target is reached, final ranking/result summary is shown and accidental continuation is prevented by absence of continuation controls.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts renders the completed bullet final result without any continuation control after settleDeal; src/ui/render.test.ts renders no human action controls once the bullet is finished; npm run verify
evidence_matrix.limitations: Continuation prevention is verified through rendered action absence and release tests, not manual keyboard tab capture.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Interactive controls remain native buttons with accessible names; discard toggles communicate selected state; finished state has no settle or next-deal controls.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/ui/render.ts actionButton/humanCards/renderContractActions/setupDiscardSelection; src/ui/render.test.ts labels discard card toggles, provides accessible names, legal actions only, finished state no controls
evidence_matrix.limitations: Keyboard reachability is inferred from native button elements plus DOM order and enabled/disabled state assertions.

downstream_notes: The score panel intentionally uses compact labeled sections and definition lists instead of a wide native table to avoid the clipping risk identified in MZ-403. If a future requirement insists on literal table semantics for the score panel, that should be a targeted UI follow-up rather than a blocker for current readability/accessibility.

No project accessibility scanner or browser tab-walk command exists in package scripts. This review therefore used the existing DOM/component/integration tests and source inspection as the smallest reliable project entrypoints.
